ety: Prk. जलणअं = S. ज्वलनीयं, rt. ज्वल्
def: v.n. To burn; to be burnt; to be on fire; to be kindled, be lighted; to be scorched, be singed; to be inflamed, to be consumed; to be touched, moved, or affected (with pity, &c.); to feel pain, sorrow, anguish, &c.; to burn or be consumed with love, or jealousy, or envy, &c.; to take amiss, be offended, be indignant; to get into a passion, be enraged, to rage
more: v. n. Pālī jalatī . 1. To burn; to be lighted; burnt. 1. Jag jalā to jalne de, maiṅ āp hī jaltī hūṅ . Prov. When I burn the world may burn. 2. Tel jale ghī, ghī jale tel . Prov. Oil burns to butter, butter burns to oil. 3. Shama kī raushnī jalte talak hai, Die * kī raushnī mahshar talak hai . Prov. Shines while it burns the candle-light, the earth-lamp's light (the light of charity) in Heaven likewise burns bright. 4. Kabīrā khaṛā bajār meṅ, lië kulhāṛī hāth, Jo ghar jāre āpnā, chale hamāre sāth . Kabīr. Kabīr stands in the market place with axe in hand, Destroy the house and we will hence to the happy land. ('Sell that thou hast and give to the poor and follow me.'). 5. Hamrā joṅ janmal ke dekhī kai jarve, toṅ sadāë jarite din jatau! Tir. 6. Lagat bhalī, bichhrat burī, jalo baro yeh rīt, Kin sukh pāyo, rī sakhī, pardesī kī prīt .? Dohā. How sweet to love, what pain to part, fire burn su…

v. n. To burn, to be burnt, to be kindled; met. to get into a passion, to be enraged, to rage. جل اٿهنا jal uṭhnā, To break out (as, fire). جل بجهنا jal bujhnā, To brand, to burn to ashes. جل پکنا jal paknā, To be in a passion, to rage. جلي پرنون لگانا jale par non lagānā (lit. to throw salt on one who is burned) To triumph over, to insult.
subs: v.n. To break out (a fire); to blaze up
v.n. To burn to ashes, to burn out; to brand
v.n. To be in a passion, to rage
v.n. (intens.) To be burnt up, be consumed (with, -se), &c., see jalnā
v.n. To die by burning; to burn (oneself) to death.
wety: Urdu verb set جَلْنا (jalnā) جَلانا (jalānā) جَلْوانا (jalvānā) Etymology tree Proto-Indo-European *ǵwelH- Sanskrit ज्वल् (jval) Sanskrit ज्वलति (jvalati) Prakrit 𑀚𑀮𑀇 (jalaï) Urdu جَلْنا Inherited from Middle Hindi جلنا (jlna /⁠jlna⁠/) (c. 1649), from Prakrit 𑀚𑀮𑀇 (jalaï), from Sanskrit ज्वलति (jválati). def: s.m. Manifestation, publicity, conspicuousness; splendour, lustre, effulgence; displaying a bride (to her husband) unveiled and in all her ornaments; the meeting of the bride and bridegroom (in the presence of their relations); the ceremony (among Mohammadans) when the bridegroom reads a chapter of the Qorʼān, and for the first time sees his wife's face in a mirror (syn. ārsī-muṣḥaf); — the nuptial bed; the bridal ornaments
more: n. m. 1. Lustre ( bhaṛak , 1, 2.) 2. The unveiling of the bride in her bridal array before the bridegroom. jal'vā , n. m. See ārsī mus'haf . jalve kā gīt , n. m. Epithalamium.

(or jilwa ) s. m. 1. Splendour, lustre. 2. The bridal bed or ornaments. جلوه گاه jalwa-gāh, s. f. Nuptial throne, place of display. جلوه گر jalwa-gar, adj. Splendid, conspicuous. جلوه گري jalwa-gar-ī, s. f. 1. Blandishments, affectation. 2. Conspicuity, splendour.
subs: adj. & adv. Clear, manifest, conspicuous, splendid; — conspicuously, splendidly
s.f. Place of manifestation or display, theatre; nuptial throne
s.f. Clearness, conspicuousness; splendour; affectation, blandishments
adv. In public, publicly, openly (opp. to ḵẖalwat-meṅ).

ety: Pehl. yam; Zend yima; S. yama
def: s.m. Name of an ancient king of Persia ( = jam-shed).
more: n. m. S. यम The regent of the nether worlds and the judge of departed souls; the Pluto of the Hindus; the angel of death. 1. Bauhre kī 'Rām Rām,' Jam kā saṅdesā . Prov. A creditor's 'Good-morning' is as welcome as a summons from the Angel of death. 2. Tua pad paṅkaj chhārī āur nahīṅ jānchië, He! sudishṭ herahu ek berī yam soṅ bāṅchië! Tir. Thy lotus feet I will not leave, of thee alone I crave, One look of grace on me bestow, from flames of Hell but save! 2. Anything intolerable or irksome. jam-dutyā , n. m. The second day in the light half of the month Kātik. On this day brothers and sisters dress up and exchange gifts and compliments, in allusion to the attachment of Yam and his sister Yamunā . Bate. jam-dhar , n. m. A dagger. jam-diyā, jam-divārī , n. m. A lamp consecrated to Jam , lighted on the 13th of Kārtik, krishn-paksh , or three days before the Diwālī. jam-rāj, jam-rāë , n. m. …
n. prop. Name of an ancient king of Persia. See جمشيد jamshīd.

s. m. The regent of worlds below; or death, and judge of departed souls. جم دوت यमदूत jam-dūt, Messenger of Jam or of death. جم ديا jam-diyā ( यमदीप ) s. m. A candle or illumination sacred to Jam, lighted on the 13th of kārtik, kr̤ishṇ paksh̤, i. e. three days before the dewālī.
s. (dakh.) The whole, all: see جمع jamﻌ.

jam-rāʼe, s.m. The king Jam, the king of Hell, or of death.

A collection, a number together, an assemblage, a congregation, assembly; conjunction; accumulation; aggregate, amount, sum total, whole; (in Arith.) addition; the plural (of a word), the plural number; capital, principal, stock, assets; a fund; outlay, cost price; the credit side of an account, credit; collections, receipts; proceeds of land; the land tax, the government demand, revneue (of the state)

Apnī aqal, parāī jama bahut hī mālūm hotī hai . Prov. One's own sense and another's wealth seem very great. 4. Outlay; cost price. 5. Receipts; collections. 6. Receipts and disbursements; revenue receipts and balances; debit and credit; cash account;…

ety: having from eleven to thirty breadths of cloth in the skirt, which at the upper part is folded into innumerable plaits, and the body part, being double-breasted, is tied in two places on each side
def: s.m. A wardrobe, a chest or sack for holding clothes; — jāma-dār, s.m. Keeper of a wardrobe; guard; — jāma-dār-ḵẖāna, s.m. = jāma-ḵẖāna
more: n. m. 1. A robe; gown. 2. A long gown, having from eleven to thirty breadths of cloth in the skirt, which at the upper part is folded into innumerable plaits and the body part being double-breasted is tied in two places on each side. It is worn especially by the bridegroom at weddings, and also by old men. jāmah-zeb , adj. Becoming; conferring grace or beauty. jāme meṅ phūlā na samānā , v. n. To be so swollen with joy that one's clothes cannot contain him; to jump out of one's skin for joy. jāme se bāhar honā , v. n. 1. To be unable to contain or control oneself; to be in an ungovernable rage. 2. To be transported with joy. (Rare) .

s. m. A garment, robe, gown, vest. جامه خانه jāma-ḵẖāna, s. m. A wardrobe, a chest or sack for holding clothes. جامه داني jāma-dānī, s. f. Portmanteau. جامه زيب jāma-zeb, adj. Becoming oneʼs clothes. جامه وار jāma-wār, 1. adj. Fit to make clothes of, sufficient for a jāma. 2. s. m. A flowered sheet or shawl, a kind of chintz.

s. m. A long gown (having from eleven to thirty breadths of cloth in the skirt, which at the upper part is folded into innumerable plaits, and the body part, being double-breasted, is tied in two places on each side: see Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: s.f. A company, body, band, party, troop, group, congregated or collective body, assembly, congregation, society, meeting, gathering; class, order, rank
more: n. f. جمع he collected. 1. A party of men; a company; corporation; body; society; community. Jamāt karāmāt . Prov. Miracles in numbers. 2. A meeting; gathering; assembly; convention; congregation; band; faction. 3. Companies or crafts under hereditary chiefs who, with a panchāyat , settle all disputes among themselves, including those of caste. Carnegy . 4. A religious service. Jamāat ho gaī, yā nahīṅ? 5. Group; class; rank; order; a class in a school. 6. ( Mah .) A congregation at prayer in a mosque. jamāٴat bilā sanad , G. G. Unregistered company. jamāat-i-sanad-yāftah , G. G. An incorporated company; a corporate body. jamāat-i-muttafaqa , An association. jamāat-i-mamnūa . G. G. An unlawful assembly. ham-jamāat ; H. sāddhiāī , n. m. A class-fellow.
s. f. A crowd, an assembly, a troop, meeting, society, congregation.
subs: s.m. An incorporated company, a corporate body

ety: jamak˚ = Prk. जम्मक्क(इ) or जम्मक्के(इ), fr. S. जन्म+कृ; see jamnā
def: v.n. To be collected, be assembled; to be settled or adjusted; to succeed, go on well; to fit; to flourish, prosper, thrive ( = jamnā; panapnā; ćamaknā); to be going on, be proceeding, be begun (as a case or suit, e.g. mugaddama jamkā); to be joined (as battle, e.g. laṛāʼī jamkī); to be full (as a meeting, &c.; e.g. majlis jamkī)
more: v. n. To succeed, to go on well, to fit, to be assembled. دوکان جمکي dūkān jamkī, Buyers and sellers are collected in the shop. لڙاٴي جمکي laṛāʼī jamkī, The fight was joined. مجلس جمکي majlis jamkī, The assembly is full. مقدمه جمکا muḳaddama jamkā, The cause is begun to be debated.
subs: Buyers and sellers are collected in the shop.

n. f. 1. Life; spirit; soul. Tumhārī jān kī qasam! By your life! (As I love you). 2. Vitality; animation; the breath of life. 1. Jān hai to jahān hai . Prov. While life is yours the world is yours. 2. Ek jān do qālib . Prov. 'Two seeming bodies but one heart.' Mid. N. Dream. 3. Jān kā sadqa māl, izzat kā sadqa galā . Prov. Perish your goods for life, for honor life. 4. Āī hai jān ke sāth, jāëgī janāze ke sāth . Prov. Come with your life, it leaves but with your corpse. (A bad habit). 5. Abhī to māl jokhoṅ hai, phir āge jān jokhoṅ hai . Zafar. Your goods are now in jeopardy, your life will soon in like case be. 6. Jān māl kī khair! Benediction. Health and wealth be yours! 3. Energy; vigour; force; strength ( zor ). Tumhāre badan meṅ jān to hai hī nahīṅ! 4. Pith ( ans ); marrow; sap; essence; the best part; the life and soul. 1. Khaṭāī tarkārī kī jāṅ hai . Acids are the essence of a good v…

s. f. m. 1. Life, soul, spirit, mind, self. 2. A sweetheart. 3. ( met. ) adj. Risking life, venturesome, daring, intrepid, spirited, mettled, mettlesome. جانبازي jān-bāz-ī, s. f. Risking life (in battle, &c.), intrepidity, mettle, spiritedness, working at any thing as if life depended on success. جان بازي کرنا jān-bāz-ī karnā, To risk life in a business. جان بحق تسليم هونا jān ba-ḥaḳḳ taslīm honā, To die, expire. جان بخش jān-baḵẖsh, Animating, lifegiving, forgiver of a capital crime. جان بخشي jān-baḵẖsh-ī, s. f. Giving of life, vivification, pardon of a capital crime. جان برحق تسليم کرنا jān bar ḥaḳḳ taslīm karnā, To expire, to give up the ghost. جان برهونا jān-bar honā, To survive, to outlive. جان بلب jān-ba-lab, adj. At the point of expiring. جان بلب آمده jān…

ety: jā˚ = Prk. जा(इ) or जाअ(इ) = S. या(ति), rt. या
def: v.n. To go, to depart; to pass, to pass away; to be lost, to disappear; to be ruined; to be managed, be accomplished; to arrive, reach, attain (to); to go on, to continue; to be (in which sense it is commonly used to form passive verbs)
more: v. n. S. गम् or या to go. Pr. gammaë, gamijjaë it is gone. 1. To go; go away; march away. 1. Ek raṅg ātā thā, ek jātā thā . His color came and went. 2. Jo bole so ghī ko jāë . Prov. Who speaks has to go for the butter. (Who recommends a cure has to provide it). 3. Chamṛī jāë, par damṛī na jāë . Prov. He'll part with his skin but not a farthing of his tin. 4. Sāth tere ham bhī jūṅ sāyā muqarrar jāëṅge, Āge jāëṅ, pīchhe jāëṅ, jāëṅge, par jāëṅge . Zauq. With you too I will like your shadow surely go, Before, behind, where'er you go, I'll go, I'll go. 5. Ṭake tītar gailā par, pāṅch rupaiye bhailā par . Bhoj. Prov. A penny for a partridge when the penny's gone is more than five rupees when you have it. 2. To reach; attain to. 3. To depart; set out; leave. 4. To pass; pass away ( bītnā ). 1. Din jāte der nahīṅ lagtī . Prov. Time flies swiftly. 2. Jāë hai jī nijāt ke gam meṅ, Aisī jannat gaī ja…
v. n. To go, to be, to pass, to reach, to continue. جاتا رهنا jātā rahnā, To be lost, to be missing, to disappear, vanish, to go away, to pass away.
subs: (-par), To chance to fall; to fall (on)

(-ko), To catch up, to overtake; to outstrip: (the root of jānā is frequently prefixed to other verbs, e.g. jā-baiṭhnā, To go and sit down, to sit down; — jā-ghusnā (-meṅ), To go and enter (into), to go (into); cf. the similar use of the French venir)
(permissive) v.t. To let (one) go; to liberate; to overlook, pass over, excuse (a fault); — jāne-do (imperat.), Pardon, forgive (him); let (him or it) go, let (him) alone; let it pass, never mind.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀚𑀸𑀇 (jāi), from Ashokan Prakrit 𑀬𑀸𑀢𑀺 (yāti), from Sanskrit याति॑ (yā́ti), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *yáHti, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *yáHti, from Proto-Indo-European *yéh₂ti, from *yeh₂-.

def: acknowledged, recognized, well-known, accomplished; reputed, renowned, famous

def: s.m. A created being, living being, man; person, individual; mankind; (as last member of a compound) the whole race or class of; -kind, -folk, &c.; e.g. strī-jan, 'women-kind,' 'women'; — bandhu-jan, 'kinsfolk')
more: n. m. S. जन् to be born. Man (individually or collectively); an individual. Ek ranḍī, aur itne jane! Double entendre. One woman, and so many men! jan bachcha , n. m. Offspring; family. Jan bachcha kolhū meṅ pilvā dīyā. janā , part. pass. of jannā . Born; brought forth; hence, a son. Harām kā janā sir chaṛhā jātā hai . A term of abuse. The bastard is presumptuous. jan'ī , n. f. 1. A daughter ( beṭī ). 2. A woman in general.
s. m. Man (individually or collectively), a man, person, mankind. جن بچي jan bachche, (dakh.) Offspring, family. In compos. it has a plural sense; as, स्त्रीजन strī-jan, Women.

(jana+an˚), s.m. Speaking aside (to another), secret communication, whispering
s.m. A community, nation, people; an empire, country; a province
adj. Befriending, imparting joy; inspiring with confidence; conciliating; gratifying the people: courting popular favour; — an epithet of the Deity

s.f. Hearsay, report, rumour; news, tidings, intelligence

s.m. 'The talk of men,' news, rumour, report; scandal

(or supār), adj. Resigning (one's) life into the hands of another (an epithet of a lover)

adj. Life-consuming, soul-tormenting, very painful; heart-inflaming; beloved; — jān-se jānā, v.n. To give up life, to pass away, to die
v.t. To deprive of life, to kill
v.n. To wash one's hands of life, to be weary of life; to despair of life

To be very devoted or zealous; to practise extreme diligence; to labour or strive hard, to strain every nerve

adj. Life-reducing, soul-exhausting; heart-breaking, afflicting; affecting, pathetic; — jān-kāhī, s.f. Diminution of life; what tends to shorten life
s.m., jān-kandanī, s.f., jān-kanī, s.f. The agonies of death, agony, torture
(-kī), To reduce to straits, to overcome, exhaust; to worry, tease, vex, annoy, plague, torment
(-ke sabab), To sacrifice or give up life (for)
s.f. Safety of (one's) life; life, quarter
(-ko), To hold (one) as dear as life, to deem precious, to value or prize highly
adj. Soul-melting, life-dissolving, life-destroying; baneful, pernicious; weakening; consuming; — jān-gudāzī, s.f. Dissolving or taking away of life; banefulness, &c.
(-kī), To destroy life, to kill; to worry, tease, &c. ( = jān khānā); to exert oneself to the utmost, strain every nerve
(-kī, merī), To be revived ( = jān paṛnā); to be satisfied, be comforted
adj. Scattering life; ready to sacrifice life (for another), devoted; dying in agony; — jān-nis̤ārī, s.f. Readiness to sacrifice life (for another), devotedness, devotion
v.n. Life to depart, to die

v.n. To make life wearisome; to worry; to work to death (used by women)
adj. Lifeless, dead; dispirited; listless; weak, feeble, inactive.

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جَنَّت (jannat), borrowed from Arabic جَنَّة (janna). First attested in c. 1611 as Middle Hindi جنت (jnt /⁠janat⁠/).

def: s.f. A number of men, an assemblage of people, a community; people; mankind.
ex: میرا اور ان کا گوشت پوست ایک ھے، میں جنتا کا بیٹا ھوں، خادم ھوں !
My flesh and skin and their [flesh and skin] is one, I am a son of the people, a servant!
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit जनता (janatā), from जन् (jan), from Proto-Indo-European *ǵenh₁-. Compare Spanish gente.

def: v.n. (Braj) = jānā, q.v.; — adv. & conj. = jāno, q.v. v.n. To go, to depart; to pass, to pass away; to be lost, to disappear; to be ruined; to be managed, be accomplished; to arrive, reach, attain (to); to go on, to continue; to be (in which sense it is commonly used to form passive verbs)
more: (in Braj) 1. v. n. To go: see جانا jānā. 2. See جانو jāno.

wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀚𑀡𑁆𑀡𑁄𑀯𑀯𑀻𑀅 (jaṇṇovavīa), from Sanskrit यज्ञोपवीत (yajñopavītá).

def: s.m. The sacrificial or sacred thread worn by the Brahmanical order among Hindūs (over the left shoulder and hanging down diagonally across the body to the right hip); a (thread-like) flaw in a jewel (such being regarded as sacred).
more: n. m. S. यज्ञोपवीत 1. The brahminical thread. Janeuā torā chhūūṅgī, yār bamnā ke! Song. Ṭhumrī . Your sacred string, O Brahman-born, I fain would touch! 2. A flaw in a jewel, thread-like and hence an object of worship.
s. m. 1. ( यज्ञोपवीत ) The brahmanical thread. 2. A flaw in a jewel.

more: n. f. The dūb grass; Agrostis linearis , Wat.; a fragrant grass which grows with kharīf crops.
s. f. The name of a grass ( dūb grass. Agrostis linearis).

def: s.f. = jāṅgh, q.v. s.f. The thigh.

Battle, warfare. جنگ جو jang-jo, Contentious, litigious. جنگ ديده jang-dīda, Experienced in war. جنگ وجدل jang-o-jadal, s. m. Battle, conflict, brawl, squabble, altercation.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جَنْگ (jang), from Middle Persian 𐫋𐫗𐫗𐫃 (jnng /⁠ǰang⁠/), from Proto-Indo-European *ǵʰengʰ-.

more: (contract. of جنگ Battle, and گاه Place) s. f. The field of battle.

def: s.m. The francoline partridge ( = tītar).

s.m. A jungle, wood, forest, thicket; forest land; waste land; land or country overgrown with long grass and weeds; a wild or uninhabited part; long grass and weeds (on grounds, or in a garden, &c.); — adj. Wild, waste, desert, solitary
more: n. m. S. जङ्गल. 1. A wood; forest; jungle; forest land. 1. Jaṅgal meṅ maṅgal karne-vālī! terī sadā jaë! Hin. Religious cry. Blessed ever be! thou makest the desert to rejoice and bloom. 2. Jaṅgal meṅ maṅgal, aur bastī meṅ vīrān, Jā ghar bhaṅg na sāṅchare, wo ghar bhūt masān . Bhaṅgars. Bacchanalia. In forests extasy, a city is a desert drear, The house wherein no wine cup flows blue devils do appear. 3. Jaṅgal meṅ mor nāchā kis ne dekhā? Prov. Who ever saw the peacock in the forest dance? (Said to one who boasts of the great things he did in foreign parts). 2. Waste land; untilled land. 3. Pasturage; grazing ground. jaṅgal jānā , v. n. lit . To roam in the wood. To ease oneself ( ṭaṭṭī jānā ). jaṅgal sāf k. , v. a. To clear land. jaṅgal kā ādmī , n. m. 1. A wild man. 2. Wild man of the wood; a man-monkey ( ban-mānas ). Jaṅgal kā ādmī paise ko! Street cry. One pice to see the man-monkey! …
s. m. A forest, a wood. جنگل بري jangal-burī, s. f. Act of clearing woodlands. جنگل پهرنا يا جانا jangal-phirnā, or -jānā (lit. To walk in or go to the wood) v. n. To ease oneʼs self.
subs: s.f. The act of clearing woodlands
jangal jānā, v.n. 'To walk in or go to the wood'; to ease oneself
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀚𑀁𑀕𑀮 (jaṃgala), from Sanskrit जङ्गल (jaṅgala, “arid or sterile region”), from a substrate language.

def: s.m. corr. of zangār, q.v. s.m. Verdigris; rust; (in comp.) acetate; sulphate, &c.

adj. & s.m. Wild; woody; — woody country, forest; a coppice, thicket; a grating, lattice; railing, fence; cloth pencilled with groups of trees, flowers, &c., in various colours; — name of a rāginī or musical mode; a medley of tunes, a Dutch concert
more: n. m. 1. A coppice; thicket. 2. A fence; railing; grating; lattice. 3. Cloth pencilled with groups of trees, flowers, etc. in various colours. 4. A medley of tunes sung together; a Dutch concert; a rāgnī , q. v. jaṅglā lagānā , v. a. To fence or rail.
1. adj. Wild; s. m. Woody country, forest. 2. s. m. Name of a rāginī or musical mode.
subs: (meṅ), To put in a grating or lattice; to put up a railing, to fence.

adj. Rural, picturesque, diversified with hill, vale, wood and water; — wild, not tame, savage, &c. ( = jaṅglī); — s.m. Picturesque country or scenery.

adj. & s.m. Moving, movable, locomotive; in motion; having motion (opp. to stationary), having life, living; — the animal kingdom; a wanderer; a wandering mendicant, a class of mendicants with matted hair, who wear a thin chain on their feet and carry a bell which they keep constantly ringing.
more: adj. S. गम् to go. Capable of locomotion; what has life and movement.
n. m. 1. The animal kingdom. 2. A class of faqīrs with matted hair and a thin chain to their feet. Jogī, atīt, jaṅgam yā seoṛā kahāyā, Yā khol-ke jaṭā ko, yā ghoṭ sir muṅḍāyā . Nazīr.
s. m. A kind of faḳīr who has matted hair and rings a bell.
adj. Removable, moveable, in motion, mendicant.

(comp. of جنگ and مشت) s. f. Boxing.

more: E. n. m. S. जङ्धा 1. The limbs. Jāṅgar māṅ ghūn laglahu, uṭhī nai hoichhhu jhat dui? Tir. Wom. Have you caught the foul disease that you cant get up. (Said to a lazy person.) 2. Strength; muscular strength; vigour. Jab tak jāṅgar chalte haiṅ, karte haiṅ . jāṅgar toṛnā , v. n. To labor hard for one's living. jāṅgar thaknā , W.; jāṅgh khasab , v. n. To become feeble and unfit for work by reason of age.
s. m. The thigh and leg.
subs: v.n. lit. 'To break the legs'; to wear the legs out, to labour or toil hard, to drudge.

def: s.m. A kind of heron, Ardea indica; the snake-bird, Plotus melanogaster. (The long scapular feathers of the Ardea are used as a badge of royalty among the Khasias, and were once used as a badge by one of the Bengal Irreg. Cavalry regiments.)
more: E. n. m. S. जाङ्गल 1. A kind of heron ( Ardea Indica ). 2. The snake bird; Plotus mel anogaster . ☞ They often float beneath the water shewing only the head and neck. They build in trees in the rains and lay elongated white eggs. Their long scapular feathers, which are shining black, with a white line down the centre, are badges of royalty among the Khasias and were used as a badge by one of the Bengal Irregular Cavalry Regiments.
s. m. A kind of heron. (Ardea Indica).

s.m. The name of a large and turbulent tribe of Rājpūts.

more: n. m. Short breeches reaching half way down the thighs; short drawers.
s. m. A kind of breeches that do not cover the thigh.
